Functions for drawing and rendering text boxes in the health UI.

# Purpose
This header file declares two functions related to rendering text in a user interface. The function [`health_ui_draw_text_in_box`](<#health_ui_draw_text_in_box>) is used to draw text within a specified rectangular area (`GRect`) on a graphical context (`GContext`), with parameters for text offset, font, and colors for both the box and text. The function [`health_ui_render_typical_text_box`](<#health_ui_render_typical_text_box>) is designed to render a standard text box on a given layer, using a specified text value. The file includes the `ui.h` header from the `applib` library, indicating that it relies on user interface components defined elsewhere.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/ui.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### health\_ui\_draw\_text\_in\_box<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_ui_draw_text_in_box}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_ui.h#L21>)

Draws text within a specified box on a graphics context.
- **Description**: Use this function to render text inside a defined rectangular area on a graphics context. It centers the text horizontally and applies a vertical offset as specified. The function also allows you to set the background color of the text box and the color of the text itself. Ensure that the graphics context is valid and initialized before calling this function. The text will not be drawn if the specified text color is `GColorClear`, and the box will not be filled if the box color is `GColorClear`. This function is useful for displaying text with specific styling in a user interface.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` object where the text will be drawn. Must not be null.
    - `text`: A pointer to a null-terminated string containing the text to draw. Must not be null.
    - `drawing_bounds`: A `GRect` structure defining the bounds within which the text box will be drawn. The width and height must be positive values.
    - `y_offset`: An `int16_t` value specifying the vertical offset from the top of the drawing bounds where the text box will start.
    - `small_font`: A `GFont` object representing the font to use for the text. Must be a valid font.
    - `box_color`: A `GColor` value specifying the color to fill the text box. Use `GColorClear` to avoid filling the box.
    - `text_color`: A `GColor` value specifying the color of the text. Use `GColorClear` to avoid drawing the text.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_ui_draw_text_in_box`](<health_ui.c.md#health_ui_draw_text_in_box>)  (Implementation)


---
### health\_ui\_render\_typical\_text\_box<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_ui_render_typical_text_box}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_ui.h#L25>)

Renders a text box with typical and value text on a specified layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to draw a text box on a given `Layer` with a typical text label and a value text. It must be called with a valid graphics context and layer. The function calculates the current weekday and displays it as part of the typical text. The text box is styled with specific background and text colors, and it uses a system font. Ensure that the `Layer` and `GContext` are properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` object. Must not be null. It represents the graphics context used for drawing.
    - `layer`: A pointer to a `Layer` object. Must not be null. It specifies the layer on which the text box will be rendered.
    - `value_text`: A pointer to a null-terminated string. Must not be null. It contains the text to display below the typical text in the text box.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_ui_render_typical_text_box`](<health_ui.c.md#health_ui_render_typical_text_box>)  (Implementation)
